person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Renard IV de Joigny or Renaud IV de Joigny (born c. 1120/1130, † c. 1179) is Count of Joigny, in Champagne. He is probably the son of Renard III de Joigny, Comte de Joigny, and Alix de Blois, daughter of Étienne II de Blois and Adele of England (it is also possible that his mother was Wandalmode de Beaujeu).

«b»Biography«/b» 
In 1147, he accompanied King Louis the Younger to the Second Crusade with his uncle Gui de Joigny. He returned to Joigny around 1150.

He became Count of Joigny on the death of his father around 1154.

In 1161, on the death of the Count of Nevers Guillaume III, he joined forces with Étienne I, Count of Sancerre, against Guillaume IV of Nevers and devastated the county of Nevers before being beaten in the17 avril 11652.

Probably after this battle, he nevertheless married the sister of Guillaume IV de Nevers: Adélaïde de Nevers.

On his death in 1179 at the latest, he was buried in the priory of Joigny.

«b»Marriage and children «/b»
He married Adélaïde de Nevers, daughter of Guillaume III de Nevers, Count of Nevers, Auxerre and Tonnerre, and Ide de Sponheim (daughter of Engelbert de Sponheim , Duke of Carinthia, and Uta de Passau) whose he has four known children:

1.) Guillaume I of Joigny , who succeeded his father.

2.) Gaucher de Joigny , Lord of Chateau-Renard.

3.) Agnès de Joigny, who married Simon de Broyes, Lord of Beaufort, with whom she had a daughter (Félicité de Broyes), then in second marriage Henri d'Arzillières.

4.) Hélisende de Joigny, who married Jean de Montréal, Lord of Arcis-sur-Aube, with whom she had three children (Jean d'Arcis-sur-Aube, Anséric d'Arcis-sur-Aube and Gui d'Arcis-sur- Aube), then in second marriage Milon du Puiset, Count of Bar-sur-Seine, with whom she had three children (Hugues du Puiset, Gaucher du Puiset and a girl who would have become a nun at the Paraclete Abbey. Some 19th century historians  century mentioned Guillaume de Chartres, fourteenth master of the Order of the Temple as being his son but this is probably an error.)

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Renard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from ragin ‘advice, decision’ + hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’. In French, renard has become the generic term for a fox, as a result of the popularity of medieval beast tales featuring Re(y)nard le goupil ‘Reynard the Fox’. The name was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain.

en.lifeSummary: When Renard de Joigny IV was born in 1120, in Joigny, Yonne, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France, his father, Renaud de Joigny, was 35 and his mother, Alix de Blois, was 21. He had at least 4 sons and 2 daughters with Adèle de Nevers. He died in 1179, in his hometown, at the age of 59.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Renard de Joigny IV est né en 1120, à Joigny, Yonne, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France, son père, Renaud de Joigny, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Alix de Blois, avait 21 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles avec Adèle de Nevers. Il est décédé en 1179, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 59 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Renard de Joigny IV 1120 in Joigny, Yonne, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Renaud de Joigny 35 und seine Mutter Alix de Blois 21. Er hatte mit Adèle de Nevers mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1179 im Alter von 59 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Renard de Joigny IV è nato il nel 1120 nel Joigny, Yonne, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Francia, il suo padre, Renaud de Joigny, aveva 35 anni e il suo madre, Alix de Blois, aveva 21 anni. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie con Adèle de Nevers. Morì nel 1179, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 59 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Renard de Joigny IVが1120年にフランスボウルゴグネ-フランチェ-コムトeイオンジョイグニーで生まれた時，父Renaud de Joignyは35歳，母Alix de Bloisは21歳であった。彼はAdèle de Neversとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1179年に故郷で59歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Renard de Joigny IV(이)가 1120년 프랑스 부르고그니-프랭체-코트e 욘 조익니에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Renaud de Joigny(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Alix de Blois(은)는 21세였다. 그는 Adèle de Nevers(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1179년 그의 고향에서 59세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Renard de Joigny IV nasceu em 1120, em Joigny, Yonne, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, França, seu pai, Renaud de Joigny, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Alix de Blois, tinha 21 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas com Adèle de Nevers. Ele faleceu em 1179, em sua cidade natal, com 59 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Renard de Joigny IV родился в 1120 г. в местности джоигни, енн, бургогне-фрэнче-комтe, Франция, его отцу по имени Renaud de Joigny было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Alix de Blois, 21. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери от Adèle de Nevers. Он умер в 1179 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 59 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Renard de Joigny IV nació en 1120, en Joigny, Yonne, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Francia, su papá, Renaud de Joigny, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Alix de Blois, tenía 21 años. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas con Adèle de Nevers. Murió en 1179, en su pueblo, a la edad de 59 años.
